_provenance: Fully synthetic illustrative policy for the browser_ops eval domain. It is NOT any real portal's, RPA tool's, or automation framework's configuration, and reproduces no real selector map, confirm-dialog behavior, retry cadence, or side-effecting-action list from any real system or prior employer. Scalar values are fabricated placeholders tagged with a <<TOKEN>> so a reviewer can see at a glance they are invented.
_schema: side_effecting_actions is a string[] of action KINDS whose re-execution is money-moving/irreversible (the leakage-sensitive double-execute set; compute.is_double_execute and the tools read it). confirm_safe_default and retry_first_response are {token, value, unit} objects; compute.py and the tools read the .value field. confirm_safe_default is the button a destructive confirm dialog defaults to; retry_first_response is the correct move on a timed-out first attempt (verify state before acting).

confirm_safe_default.note: destructive confirm dialogs default to cancel; benign/save dialogs default to confirm - varies by portal, verify per target

retry_first_response.note: on a timed-out first attempt the outcome is unknown; verify state before retrying to avoid a double-execute
